summary: This cluster of research focuses on the taxonomy, distribution, and morphology of unarmored dinoflagellates, particularly within the genera *Karenia*, *Brachidinium*, *Asterodinium*, and *Microceratium*, in the open waters of the southeastern Pacific Ocean. Key findings include the documentation of high abundances of *Karenia papilionacea* in the Marquesas Archipelago and the Peru-Chile Current, as well as observed patterns of co-occurrence and distinct morphological traits among these species. The studies provide foundational descriptions and morphometric analyses of these fragile dinoflagellates, highlighting their ecological significance in this specific geographic region.
entity_sample: pier of the Hong Kong University
